name: The Northern Flank: A Learning Scenario
source: setup from the module's own 'The Northern Flank' save; scenario rules from playbook A15.1 (GMT sanctioned Spanish translation, translated)

game.duration_cite: A15.1: from the 11:40 am turn to the 4:40 pm turn unless victory sooner

initial_lims.cite: A15.1 initial LIMs; all division leaders begin with their LIMs in the Command Pool (A15.1 special rules)

command.cite: A15.1 REGLAS ESPECIALES (GMT sanctioned Spanish translation, translated) + Command Card divisional breakpoint roster + counter art (leader ratings)

initiative_mod.cite: A15.1: initiative is rolled normally (no overall commander on map, no modifiers)

pool_management.cite: A15.1 special rule: both players may add or remove LIMs from the Command Pool voluntarily during the Command Phase of each turn (replaces Command Change rolls [4.2] for this scenario); LIMs of divisions at Breakpoint may never be added [11.2.1]

3.cite: Command Card: Suchet 8 (see source_defects AUS-CMD-1 for the 2-vs-3 numbering)

M.cite: Command Card: M/CR-Milhaud 2; A15.1: activates with the Independent LIM

T.cite: Command Card: T/CR-Treilhard 3; A15.1: activates with the Independent LIM

3v.cite: Command Card: 3 Bde/B-Voropaitzki 2; no organic units in this scenario (source_defects AUS-CMD-2)

artillery_attach.cite: A15.1 special rule: artillery of both sides may be under the command of any division leader of its corps, explicitly including the French 1/V foot battery under Suchet; neither Milhaud nor Treilhard may command artillery

victory.french_win: 7 Russian battalions/regiments/batteries destroyed, routed or unsteady
victory.russian_win: 10 French battalions/regiments/batteries destroyed, routed or unsteady
victory.draw: neither by the end of the 4:40 pm turn
victory.cite: A15.1 VICTORIA (GMT's sanctioned Spanish translation, translated); checked by the engine every turn

enforced: Formation-true movement: advance through front hexsides only; facing changes cost MPs [6.1/6.3/TEC 5.0]
TEC terrain costs per arm+formation, incl. roads (Road Movement, not in Line), streams, slopes, bridges [5.0/5.1/5.3]
Movement disorder: auto-disorder terrain and disorder checks vs morale, engine-rolled d10 [5.1.1/6.4.1]
Line special moves: slide (2x, unoccupied flank), reverse (whole MA), about face (half MA) [6.3.1/6.3.5]
Enemy front hexes end movement; no re-adjacency to the same enemy [5.1.3]
Stacking limits by type/formation/division and terrain class [7.1]
One activation per unit per game turn [3.0/4.6]
Fire combat: offensive + return fire (simultaneous), range/facing arcs/LOS, target hierarchy, once per turn [8.1.1-8.1.8]
Morale: check table + DRMs, shaken/unsteady/rout ladder, artillery morale as SP loss, chain checks on rout [9.1-9.3]
Retreats + rout retreats with SP loss for shortfall [10.0/10.1]; unit breakpoint [11.1]
Rally phase incl. routed-rally cap and rout loss [12.0-12.4]

Command system: voluntary LIM pool per A15.1, seeded initiative + LIM draws, activation rolls, Command Breakdown [4.7], full/limited activation budgets [4.6], In/Out of Command marking [4.3.3], Non-LIM phase [3.0.C], once-per-turn division rule
Division fatigue: accumulation/recovery [13.1/13.2 + designer Q&A], effects incl. 7/8/9 thresholds per errata [13.3]
Division breakpoint: LIM ban + no Full Activations [11.2.1]
enforced_tier2: Melee: Bayonet + Assault combat [8.2/8.3] with pre-shock morale checks, supporting stacks [8.2.1#2 + Fox Q&A], once-per-activation caps, melee rounds and the continue/withdraw windows [8.5/8.5.3], the errata Retreat result (2000-07-20)

Cavalry charges [8.4]: May Charge marker [5.1.2], range 2-4 + LOS + front-hexside charge path [8.4.1/8.4.2 + A8.1.1], Stand-or-Square window, charge bonus arithmetic incl. halving [A8.1.2/8.4.2#6], pursuit [8.4.2#8], Blown lifecycle [8.4.4/8.4.5]

Reaction windows [6.2]: fire/move/reverse/limber/face/charge + decline, expenditure-vs-entry triggers per arm [6.2.1-6.2.7 + Fox Q&A], once-per-activation budgets [8.1.2-8.1.4], reaction charges take the pre-melee check and end the mover's movement (Fox Q&A)

Strategic movement [5.2]: pre-action declaration, double MA, 3-hex enemy exclusion + road duties, no combat/reactions/square, marker loss on enemy approach and after combat, must-move-as-far + no-Line checks (Fox liberal reading), fatigue exemption
umpired: LIM pool secrecy (errata to 3.0.C.3/4.2): draws are engine-owned and the UI shows only your own LIMs, but the audit log necessarily records pool declarations - true concealment needs refereed (server/PBM) play
rulings: Stack order is fixed at creation order; voluntary stack reordering [7.2] is not modelled - the top unit is the first non-artillery unit placed

A reaction that changes the mover's state ends the mover's walk only on unsteady/rout/death (or any state change while further reactors are still entitled); lesser effects let the move resume [6.2 + Fox Q&A]
Free facing changes during a formation change do not take the TEC disorder check (line-in-rough-terrain edge case, not modelled)
Multi-hex counters [6.5] are out of scope - none exist in A15.1
Countercharge [8.4.2#3] is scenario-unreachable in A15.1 (the Russians field no cavalry); its arithmetic is table-validated (validate_melee.py) but has no in-game path
